title: Romance film

infobox.value: involve romantic love stories recorded in visual media for broadcast in theatres or on television that focus on [passion](/wiki/Passion_(emotion) "Passion (emotion)"), [emotion](/wiki/Emotion "Emotion"), and the affectionate romantic involvement of the main characters. Typically their journey through [dating](/wiki/Dating "Dating"), [courtship](/wiki/Courtship "Courtship") or [marriage](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") is featured. These films focus on the search for romantic love as the main plot focus. Occasionally, romance lovers face obstacles such as [finances](/wiki/Finance "Finance"), physical [illness](/wiki/Illness "Illness"), various forms of [discrimination](/wiki/Discrimination "Discrimination"), psychological restraints, or family resistance. As in all quite strong, deep, and close romantic relationships, the tensions of day-to-day life, [temptations](/wiki/Temptation "Temptation") (of [infidelity](/wiki/Infidelity "Infidelity")), and differences in compatibility enter into the plots of romantic films.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

is a term associated with romance films mostly targeted to a female audience.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) Although many romance films may be targeted at women, it is not a defining characteristic of a romance film and a '*chick flick'* does not necessarily have a romance as a central theme, revolve around the romantic involvement of characters or even include a romantic relationship. As such, the terms cannot be used interchangeably. Films of this genre include *[Gilda](/wiki/Gilda_(film) "Gilda (film)")*, *[The Red Shoes](/wiki/The_Red_Shoes_(1948_film) "The Red Shoes (1948 film)")*, *[Sense and Sensibility](/wiki/Sense_and_Sensibility_(1995_film) "Sense and Sensibility (1995 film)")*, *[Gentlemen Prefer Blondes](/wiki/Gentlemen_Prefer_Blondes_(1953_film) "Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953 film)")*, *[Dirty Dancing](/wiki/Dirty_Dancing "Dirty Dancing")*, *[The Notebook](/wiki/The_Notebook "The Notebook")*, *[Dear John](/wiki/Dear_John_(2010_film) "Dear John (2010 film)")*, *[A Walk to Remember](/wiki/A_Walk_to_Remember "A Walk to Remember"), [Thelma & Louise](/wiki/Thelma_%26_Louise "Thelma & Louise"), [Fifty Shades of Grey](/wiki/Fifty_Shades_of_Grey_(film) "Fifty Shades of Grey (film)")*, *[Sleepless in Seattle](/wiki/Sleepless_in_Seattle "Sleepless in Seattle")*,*[You've Got Mail](/wiki/You%27ve_Got_Mail "You've Got Mail")* and *[Romeo + Juliet](/wiki/Romeo_%2B_Juliet "Romeo + Juliet")*.

is a popular genre of film that features romantic relationships between humans and supernatural creatures.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Popular tropes include [vampirism](/wiki/Vampirism "Vampirism"), [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel "Time travel"), [ghosts](/wiki/Ghost "Ghost") and psychic or telekinetic abilities – i.e. things that cannot be explained by science.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The genre originated in literature and moved on to the screen in the early 2000s, following the success of the Twilight Saga adaptations from [Stephenie Meyer](/wiki/Stephenie_Meyer "Stephenie Meyer")'s books.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-8) By 2007–08, film studios were producing various paranormal romance films, many adapted from novels.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-8)

are films with light-hearted, humorous plotlines centered on romantic ideals such as true love being able to surmount most obstacles. Humour in such films tends to be of a verbal, low-key variety or situational, as opposed to slapstick.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Films within this genre include *[City Lights](/wiki/City_Lights "City Lights")*, *[A Night at the Opera](/wiki/A_Night_at_the_Opera_(film) "A Night at the Opera (film)")*, *[It Happened One Night](/wiki/It_Happened_One_Night "It Happened One Night")*, *[His Girl Friday](/wiki/His_Girl_Friday "His Girl Friday")*, *[My Wife's Goblin](/wiki/My_Wife%27s_Goblin "My Wife's Goblin")*, *[The Philadelphia Story](/wiki/The_Philadelphia_Story_(film) "The Philadelphia Story (film)")*, *[Intolerable Cruelty](/wiki/Intolerable_Cruelty "Intolerable Cruelty")*, *[Roman Holiday](/wiki/Roman_Holiday "Roman Holiday")*, *[Good Morning, My Dear Wife](/wiki/Good_Morning,_My_Dear_Wife "Good Morning, My Dear Wife")*, *[The Big Sick](/wiki/The_Big_Sick "The Big Sick")*, *[Enough Said](/wiki/Enough_Said "Enough Said")*, *[Lost In Translation](/wiki/Lost_in_Translation_(film) "Lost in Translation (film)")*, *[To All the Boys I've Loved Before](/wiki/To_All_the_Boys_I%27ve_Loved_Before_(film) "To All the Boys I've Loved Before (film)")*, *[Four Weddings and a Funeral](/wiki/Four_Weddings_and_a_Funeral "Four Weddings and a Funeral")*, *[Dave](/wiki/Dave_(film) "Dave (film)")*, *[Say Anything...](/wiki/Say_Anything... "Say Anything...")*, *[Moonstruck](/wiki/Moonstruck "Moonstruck")*, *[In Summer We Must Love](/wiki/In_Summer_We_Must_Love "In Summer We Must Love")*, *[As Good as It Gets](/wiki/As_Good_as_It_Gets "As Good as It Gets")*, *[Something's Gotta Give](/wiki/Something%27s_Gotta_Give_(film) "Something's Gotta Give (film)")*, *[When Harry Met Sally...](/wiki/When_Harry_Met_Sally... From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Film genre

"Love film" redirects here. For the UK-based video service, see [LoveFilm](/wiki/LoveFilm "LoveFilm").

For other uses, see [Romance (disambiguation)](/wiki/Romance_(disambiguation) "Romance (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9e/Tyrone_power_alice_faye_ragtime6.jpg/330px-Tyrone_power_alice_faye_ragtime6.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tyrone_power_alice_faye_ragtime6.jpg)

[Tyrone Power](/wiki/Tyrone_Power "Tyrone Power") passionately embraces [Alice Faye](/wiki/Alice_Faye "Alice Faye") in the [1938 film](/wiki/1938_in_film "1938 in film") *[Alexander's Ragtime Band](/wiki/Alexander%27s_Ragtime_Band_(film) "Alexander's Ragtime Band (film)")*.

**Romance films** involve romantic love stories recorded in visual media for broadcast in theatres or on television that focus on [passion](/wiki/Passion_(emotion) "Passion (emotion)"), [emotion](/wiki/Emotion "Emotion"), and the affectionate romantic involvement of the main characters. Typically their journey through [dating](/wiki/Dating "Dating"), [courtship](/wiki/Courtship "Courtship") or [marriage](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") is featured. These films focus on the search for romantic love as the main plot focus. Occasionally, romance lovers face obstacles such as [finances](/wiki/Finance "Finance"), physical [illness](/wiki/Illness "Illness"), various forms of [discrimination](/wiki/Discrimination "Discrimination"), psychological restraints, or family resistance. As in all quite strong, deep, and close romantic relationships, the tensions of day-to-day life, [temptations](/wiki/Temptation "Temptation") (of [infidelity](/wiki/Infidelity "Infidelity")), and differences in compatibility enter into the plots of romantic films.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Romantic films often explore the essential themes of [love at first sight](/wiki/Love_at_first_sight "Love at first sight"), young and mature love, unrequited love, obsession, sentimental love, [spiritual love](/wiki/Spirituality "Spirituality"), forbidden love, platonic love, sexual and passionate love, sacrificial love, explosive and destructive love, and [tragic love](/wiki/Tragedy "Tragedy"). Romantic films serve as great escapes and fantasies for viewers, especially if the two leads finally overcome their difficulties, declare their love, and experience their "happily ever after", often implied by a reunion and final kiss. In romantic television series, such romantic relationships may develop over many episodes or different characters may become intertwined in different romantic arcs.

Screenwriter and scholar [Eric R. Williams](/wiki/Eric_R._Williams "Eric R. Williams") identifies Romance Films as one of eleven super-genres in his [screenwriters' taxonomy](/wiki/Screenwriters_Taxonomy "Screenwriters Taxonomy"), claiming that all feature-length narrative films can be classified by these super-genres. The other ten super-genres are [action](/wiki/Action_film "Action film"), [crime](/wiki/Crime_film "Crime film"), [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_film "Fantasy film"), [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction_film "Science fiction film"), [comedy](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film"), [sports](/wiki/Sports_film "Sports film"), [thriller](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film"), [war](/wiki/War_film "War film") and [western](/wiki/Western_(genre) "Western (genre)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Decline in romance films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Decline in romance films")]

The proportion of romance films within the total number of movies produced has significantly declined in recent years. According to data from [IMDb](/wiki/IMDb "IMDb"), the share of romance films decreased from 34.8% of all movies released in 2000 to 8.6% in the most recent year reported.[[3]](#cite_note-c759-3)

Romance films have experienced a cyclical presence in cinema history. They constituted a substantial portion of the movies released from the 1940s through the early 1960s, followed by a notable decline. The genre experienced a gradual recovery over the last three decades of the 20th century, peaking in the early 2000s. However, this trend has reversed in more recent times. Moreover, the integration of romance as a subplot in films of other genres, such as [action movies](/wiki/Action_film "Action film"), also appears to be diminishing.[[3]](#cite_note-c759-3)

### Chick flick

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Chick flick")]

Main article: [Chick flick](/wiki/Chick_flick "Chick flick")

**'Chick flick'** is a term associated with romance films mostly targeted to a female audience.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) Although many romance films may be targeted at women, it is not a defining characteristic of a romance film and a '*chick flick'* does not necessarily have a romance as a central theme, revolve around the romantic involvement of characters or even include a romantic relationship. As such, the terms cannot be used interchangeably. Films of this genre include *[Gilda](/wiki/Gilda_(film) "Gilda (film)")*, *[The Red Shoes](/wiki/The_Red_Shoes_(1948_film) "The Red Shoes (1948 film)")*, *[Sense and Sensibility](/wiki/Sense_and_Sensibility_(1995_film) "Sense and Sensibility (1995 film)")*, *[Gentlemen Prefer Blondes](/wiki/Gentlemen_Prefer_Blondes_(1953_film) "Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953 film)")*, *[Dirty Dancing](/wiki/Dirty_Dancing "Dirty Dancing")*, *[The Notebook](/wiki/The_Notebook "The Notebook")*, *[Dear John](/wiki/Dear_John_(2010_film) "Dear John (2010 film)")*, *[A Walk to Remember](/wiki/A_Walk_to_Remember "A Walk to Remember"), [Thelma & Louise](/wiki/Thelma_%26_Louise "Thelma & Louise"), [Fifty Shades of Grey](/wiki/Fifty_Shades_of_Grey_(film) "Fifty Shades of Grey (film)")*, *[Sleepless in Seattle](/wiki/Sleepless_in_Seattle "Sleepless in Seattle")*,*[You've Got Mail](/wiki/You%27ve_Got_Mail "You've Got Mail")* and *[Romeo + Juliet](/wiki/Romeo_%2B_Juliet "Romeo + Juliet")*.

### Historical romance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Historical romance")]

Main article: [Historical romance](/wiki/Historical_romance "Historical romance")

Also known as epic romance, this is a romantic story with a historical period setting, normally with a turbulent backdrop of war, revolution, or tragedy. This includes films such as *[Gone with the Wind](/wiki/Gone_with_the_Wind_(film) "Gone with the Wind (film)")*, *[Doctor Zhivago](/wiki/Doctor_Zhivago_(film) "Doctor Zhivago (film)")*, *[Reds](/wiki/Reds_(film) "Reds (film)")*, *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)")*, *[A Very Long Engagement](/wiki/A_Very_Long_Engagement "A Very Long Engagement")*, *[Atonement](/wiki/Atonement_(2007_film) "Atonement (2007 film)")*, *[Portrait of a Lady on Fire](/wiki/Portrait_of_a_Lady_on_Fire "Portrait of a Lady on Fire")*, and *[Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War_(2018_film) "Cold War (2018 film)")*.

### Paranormal romance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Paranormal romance")]

Main article: [Paranormal romance](/wiki/Paranormal_romance "Paranormal romance")

**Paranormal romance** is a popular genre of film that features romantic relationships between humans and supernatural creatures.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Popular tropes include [vampirism](/wiki/Vampirism "Vampirism"), [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel "Time travel"), [ghosts](/wiki/Ghost "Ghost") and psychic or telekinetic abilities – i.e. things that cannot be explained by science.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The genre originated in literature and moved on to the screen in the early 2000s, following the success of the Twilight Saga adaptations from [Stephenie Meyer](/wiki/Stephenie_Meyer "Stephenie Meyer")'s books.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-8) By 2007–08, film studios were producing various paranormal romance films, many adapted from novels.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-8)

Examples of paranormal romance films include *[The Twilight Saga](/wiki/The_Twilight_Saga_(film_series) "The Twilight Saga (film series)")*, *[Warm Bodies](/wiki/Warm_Bodies_(film) "Warm Bodies (film)")*, *[Vampire Academy](/wiki/Vampire_Academy_(film) "Vampire Academy (film)")*, *[I Am Dragon](/wiki/He%27s_a_Dragon "He's a Dragon")*, and *[The Shape of Water](/wiki/The_Shape_of_Water "The Shape of Water")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Romantic comedy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Romantic comedy")]

Main article: [Romantic comedy](/wiki/Romantic_comedy "Romantic comedy")

**Romantic comedies** are films with light-hearted, humorous plotlines centered on romantic ideals such as true love being able to surmount most obstacles. Humour in such films tends to be of a verbal, low-key variety or situational, as opposed to slapstick.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Films within this genre include *[City Lights](/wiki/City_Lights "City Lights")*, *[A Night at the Opera](/wiki/A_Night_at_the_Opera_(film) "A Night at the Opera (film)")*, *[It Happened One Night](/wiki/It_Happened_One_Night "It Happened One Night")*, *[His Girl Friday](/wiki/His_Girl_Friday "His Girl Friday")*, *[My Wife's Goblin](/wiki/My_Wife%27s_Goblin "My Wife's Goblin")*, *[The Philadelphia Story](/wiki/The_Philadelphia_Story_(film) "The Philadelphia Story (film)")*, *[Intolerable Cruelty](/wiki/Intolerable_Cruelty "Intolerable Cruelty")*, *[Roman Holiday](/wiki/Roman_Holiday "Roman Holiday")*, *[Good Morning, My Dear Wife](/wiki/Good_Morning,_My_Dear_Wife "Good Morning, My Dear Wife")*, *[The Big Sick](/wiki/The_Big_Sick "The Big Sick")*, *[Enough Said](/wiki/Enough_Said "Enough Said")*, *[Lost In Translation](/wiki/Lost_in_Translation_(film) "Lost in Translation (film)")*, *[To All the Boys I've Loved Before](/wiki/To_All_the_Boys_I%27ve_Loved_Before_(film) "To All the Boys I've Loved Before (film)")*, *[Four Weddings and a Funeral](/wiki/Four_Weddings_and_a_Funeral "Four Weddings and a Funeral")*, *[Dave](/wiki/Dave_(film) "Dave (film)")*, *[Say Anything...](/wiki/Say_Anything... "Say Anything...")*, *[Moonstruck](/wiki/Moonstruck "Moonstruck")*, *[In Summer We Must Love](/wiki/In_Summer_We_Must_Love "In Summer We Must Love")*, *[As Good as It Gets](/wiki/As_Good_as_It_Gets "As Good as It Gets")*, *[Something's Gotta Give](/wiki/Something%27s_Gotta_Give_(film) "Something's Gotta Give (film)")*, *[When Harry Met Sally...](/wiki/When_Harry_Met_Sally... ### Romantic drama

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Romantic drama")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/08/Salah_Zulfikar%26Shadia.jpg/250px-Salah_Zulfikar%26Shadia.jpg)](/wiki/File:Salah_Zulfikar%26Shadia.jpg)

[Salah Zulfikar](/wiki/Salah_Zulfikar "Salah Zulfikar") passionately embracing [Shadia](/wiki/Shadia "Shadia") in the [1965 film](/wiki/1965_in_film "1965 in film") *[Dearer than my Life](/wiki/Aghla_Min_Hayati "Aghla Min Hayati")*

**Romantic dramas** usually revolve around an obstacle that prevents deep and true love between two people. Music is often employed to indicate the emotional mood, creating an atmosphere of greater insulation for the couple. The conclusion of a romantic drama typically does not indicate whether a final romantic union between the two main characters will occur.

Some examples of romantic drama films and shows before 2000 are *[Man's Way with Women](/wiki/Man%27s_Way_with_Women "Man's Way with Women")*, *[Casablanca](/wiki/Casablanca_(film) "Casablanca (film)")*, *[María Candelaria](/wiki/Mar%C3%ADa_Candelaria "María Candelaria")*, *[Pride & Prejudice](/wiki/Pride_%26_Prejudice_(2005_film) "Pride & Prejudice (2005 film)")*, *[Appointment with Happiness](/wiki/Appointment_with_Happiness "Appointment with Happiness")*, *[Turkish Delight (1973 film)](/wiki/Turkish_Delight_(1973_film) "Turkish Delight (1973 film)")*, *[Wakeful Eyes](/wiki/Wakeful_Eyes "Wakeful Eyes")*, *[Among the Ruins](/wiki/Among_the_Ruins "Among the Ruins")*, *[The River of Love](/wiki/The_River_of_Love_(1960_film) "The River of Love (1960 film)")*, *[Dearer than my Life](/wiki/Aghla_Min_Hayati "Aghla Min Hayati")*, *[Love Story](/wiki/Love_Story_(1970_film) "Love Story (1970 film)")*, *[Paris and Love](/wiki/Paris_and_Love "Paris and Love")*, *[Featureless Men](/wiki/Featureless_Men "Featureless Men")*, *[Coming Home](/wiki/Coming_Home_(1978_film) "Coming Home (1978 film)")*, *[Daughters of the Dust](/wiki/Daughters_of_the_Dust "Daughters of the Dust")*, *[Like Water for Chocolate](/wiki/Like_Water_for_Chocolate_(film) "Like Water for Chocolate (film)")*, *[Sommersby](/wiki/Sommersby "Sommersby")*, *[The Bridges of Madison County](/wiki/The_Bridges_of_Madison_County_(film) "The Bridges of Madison County (film)")*, *[The English Patient](/wiki/The_English_Patient_(film) "The English Patient (film)")*, *[Shakespeare in Love](/wiki/Shakespeare_in_Love "Shakespeare in Love")*, *[An Officer and a Gentleman](/wiki/An_Officer_and_a_Gentleman "An Officer and a Gentleman")*,  *[Saptapadi](/wiki/Saptapadi_(1961_film) "Saptapadi (1961 film)")*, *[Hello, That's Me!](/wiki/Hello,_That%27s_Me! "Hello, That's Me!")* and *[Cinema Paradiso](/wiki/Cinema_Paradiso "Cinema Paradiso")*.

21st century examples include *[Kabhi Alvida Naa Kehna](/wiki/Kabhi_Alvida_Naa_Kehna "Kabhi Alvida Naa Kehna")*, *[Sideways](/wiki/Sideways "Sideways")*, *[Memoirs of a Geisha](/wiki/Memoirs_of_a_Geisha_(film) "Memoirs of a Geisha (film)")*, *[Slumdog Millionaire](/wiki/Slumdog_Millionaire "Slumdog Millionaire")*, *[Up in the Air](/wiki/Up_in_the_Air_(2009_film) "Up in the Air (2009 film)")*, *[The Artist](/wiki/The_Artist_(film) "The Artist (film)")*, *[Gloria Bell](/wiki/Gloria_Bell "Gloria Bell")*, and *[Malcolm & Marie](/wiki/Malcolm_%26_Marie "Malcolm & Marie")*.

*[Kasautii Zindagii Kay](/wiki/Kasautii_Zindagii_Kay_(2001_TV_series) "Kasautii Zindagii Kay (2001 TV series)")* is an Indian romantic drama television series of the 2000s.

### Romantic fantasy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Romantic fantasy")]

Main article: [Romantic fantasy](/wiki/Romantic_fantasy "Romantic fantasy")

**Romantic fantasies** describe [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") stories using many of the elements and conventions of the romance genre. ### Romantic musical

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Romantic musical")]

**Romantic musical** (alternatively **musical romance**) is a genre of film that features romantic relationships and whose story is partially explained through song or dance numbers. This genre originated on Broadway and moved to the silver screen thanks in part to the popularity of the [Rodgers and Hammerstein](/wiki/Rodgers_and_Hammerstein "Rodgers and Hammerstein") productions.

Some examples include *[South Pacific](/wiki/South_Pacific_(1958_film) "South Pacific (1958 film)")*, *[West Side Story](/wiki/West_Side_Story_(1961_film) "West Side Story (1961 film)")* and its [2021 remake](/wiki/West_Side_Story_(2021_film) "West Side Story (2021 film)"), *[Grease](/wiki/Grease_(film) "Grease (film)")*, *[High School Musical](/wiki/High_School_Musical "High School Musical")*, *[Across the Universe](/wiki/Across_the_Universe_(film) "Across the Universe (film)")*, *[Moulin Rouge!](/wiki/Moulin_Rouge! "Moulin Rouge!")*, the *[Mamma Mia!](/wiki/Mamma_Mia!_(film_series) "Mamma Mia! (film series)")* franchise, *[Sunshine on Leith](/wiki/Sunshine_on_Leith_(film) "Sunshine on Leith (film)")*, and *[La La Land](/wiki/La_La_Land "La La Land")*.

### Romantic thriller

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Romantic thriller")]

Main article: [Romantic thriller](/wiki/Romantic_thriller "Romantic thriller")

**Romantic thriller** is a film genre with a storyline combining elements of the romance film and the [thriller genre](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film"). Some examples of romantic thriller films are *[Desire](/wiki/Desire_(1936_film) "Desire (1936 film)")*, *[To Catch a Thief](/wiki/To_Catch_a_Thief "To Catch a Thief")*, *[Vertigo](/wiki/Vertigo_(film) "Vertigo (film)")*, *[The Crying Game](/wiki/The_Crying_Game "The Crying Game")*, *[The Bodyguard](/wiki/The_Bodyguard_(1992_film) "The Bodyguard (1992 film)")*, *[Unfaithful](/wiki/Unfaithful_(2002_film) "Unfaithful (2002 film)")*, *[Wicker Park](/wiki/Wicker_Park_(film) "Wicker Park (film)")*, *[The Phantom of the Opera](/wiki/The_Phantom_of_the_Opera_(2004_film) "The Phantom of the Opera (2004 film)")*, *[The Tourist](/wiki/The_Tourist_(2010_film) "The Tourist (2010 film)")*, and *[The Adjustment Bureau](/wiki/The_Adjustment_Bureau "The Adjustment Bureau")*.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Film types, macro genres and the filmmaker's voice

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Romance_film&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Film types, macro genres and the filmmaker's voice")]

The [screenwriters taxonomy](/wiki/Screenwriters_Taxonomy "Screenwriters Taxonomy") creates additional categories beyond "subgenre" when discussing films, making the argument that all narrative Hollywood films can be delineated into comedies or dramas (identified as a "film type").[[12]](#cite_note-12) The taxonomy also identifies fifty "macro genres", which can be paired with the **romance** super genre.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Using this approach, films like *[Gone with the Wind](/wiki/Gone_with_the_Wind_(film) "Gone with the Wind (film)")* (noted above) would be classified as a dramatic (type) historical/family (macro genres) romance (genre) rather than simply a historical romance; while *The Notebook* would be identified at dramatic (type) disease (macro genre) romance (genre) rather than simply a romantic drama.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Similarly, musicals are categorized as one option for a filmmaker's "voice" because the artistic choice to have the characters sing does not affect the story or the characters – it simply alters how the story and characters are conveyed.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Therefore, a romance film like *[Grease](/wiki/Grease_(film) "Grease (film)")* would be categorized as a dramatic (type), romance (super genre), high school / coming of age (macro genres), musical (voice) – rather than simply as a "musical romance".[[16]](#cite_note-16)
